reasoning: A >10x spike in a single month relative to a 360-day baseline is exceedingly rare for ACLED 'Violence against civilians' data, especially when the baseline is not close to zero (current baseline reference is 10.5, meaning ~9.5 events/month). Thus, >105 events would have to occur in just 30 days. Furthermore, for the first date (Nov 2, 2025), the 30-day window heavily overlaps with the 360-day reference period (ending Oct 26, 2025), making it mathematically nearly impossible to reach a 10x spike unless all events of the preceding year occurred solely in those 30 days. While uncertainty increases slightly for the later dates, a 10x jump remains highly improbable.
